text: the mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell
photosynthesis converts sunlight into chemical energy stored in glucose
deoxyribonucleic acid carries the genetic information of living organisms
the hippocampus plays a key role in spatial navigation and memory formation
chlorophyll absorbs red and blue light to drive the process of photosynthesis
ribosomes translate messenger RNA sequences into functional proteins
natural selection acts on heritable variation within a population over time
the endoplasmic reticulum synthesizes lipids and processes proteins
adenosine triphosphate serves as the primary energy currency of the cell
meiosis produces four genetically distinct haploid daughter cells
homeostasis refers to the maintenance of a stable internal environment
the sodium potassium pump maintains the resting membrane potential of neurons
symbiosis describes a close and prolonged biological interaction between species
the cerebral cortex is responsible for higher cognitive functions in mammals
epigenetics studies heritable changes in gene expression without altering DNA
the lysosome digests cellular waste and recycles macromolecules
apoptosis is the programmed cell death pathway essential for development
the Krebs cycle oxidizes acetyl-CoA and generates electron carriers
cilia and flagella are microtubule-based structures used for cell motility
the immune system distinguishes self from non-self to prevent autoimmune reactions
phylogenetics reconstructs evolutionary relationships among organisms
the Golgi apparatus modifies sorts and packages proteins for secretion
osmosis is the passive movement of water across a semipermeable membrane
alleles are alternative forms of a gene occupying the same locus on homologous chromosomes
the nervous system transmits electrical signals through networks of neurons
biodiversity encompasses the variety of life at genetic species and ecosystem levels
convergent evolution produces similar traits in unrelated lineages through selective pressure
